response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile generalist with genuine systems-level experience, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation, and his professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments ending in 2015. His recent decade is web and product engineering in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of MQTT, message-broker architectures at industrial scale, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practice. The low-latency DLang work shows he can operate near the metal, but the core competency gap on C++/STL, industrial protocols, and security standards is too wide for a mid-senior C++ role.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, fibers, caching, millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source testing tools
Proven mentor and educator (workshops, Selenium course, refactoring dojos), matching the mentoring responsibility
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal (~7 months) and roughly a decade old
No exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0)
Career focus is web/full-stack product work, not industrial automation or networked multi-threaded C++ systems

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent hands-on C++/STL experience
No industrial cybersecurity or SDL/IEC 62443 background
Systems-level performance engineering transferable from DLang work
